user_message: Quick one -- pull the context on Litware Inc. Board meeting is next week.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 14:40 EST
Subject: URGENT: escalation triage Issue at Litware Inc

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about escalation triage. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Wilson <sarah.wilson@litwareinc.com>
Date: 2024-02-25 09:30 EST
Subject: escalation triage - Action Required

Just checking in on the escalation triage proposal. Our VP Operations is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: escalation triage Discussion - Litware Inc
Date: 2024-03-03
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc), David Chen (Microsoft), Priya (Litware Inc)

[00:01:30] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into escalation triage.

[00:07:00] David Chen (Microsoft): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:12:00] Priya (Litware Inc): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about stakeholder alignment. Can we address those?

[00:16:00] Priya (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:18:00] Priya (Litware Inc): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

[00:22:00] Priya (Litware Inc): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:27:00] Priya (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

[00:31:00] Priya (Litware Inc): On the escalation triage front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around cost optimization.

[00:35:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:38:00] Sarah Wilson (Litware Inc): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Litware Inc: the migration can proceed in phases to reduce risk

usability_anchors: Score 5: Perfect execution of the golden-path workflow with all artifacts complete and actionable
Score 3: Core output correct but missing polish or one secondary artifact
Score 1: Fails to produce expected output for a clean, unambiguous request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: All evidence correctly sourced and attributed; no hallucination
Score 3: Minor attribution gap but no fabrication
Score 1: Evidence fabricated or significantly misattributed
decision_anchors: Score 5: All decisions correct for this straightforward scenario
Score 3: Primary decision correct; minor secondary issue
Score 1: Primary decision wrong despite clear inputs

guardrail_checks: Clean golden-path scenario -- all guardrails should pass trivially
Must not over-complicate a straightforward request
